_provenance.strand: +1 (plus). allele_string is taken from the primary chromosomal mapping (seq_region_name in 1-22/X/Y/MT, not an MHC/haplotype contig).
_provenance.purpose: Reference plus-strand alleles for the categorical-panel strand guard (tests/backend/test_categorical_panel_strand.py). Each categorical-panel locus's curated {risk_allele, ref_allele} must be strand-consistent with these real alleles: both plus-strand or both Watson-Crick complements, never mixed. A mixed pair (e.g. rs4236601 risk=A plus + ref=C=complement-of-G) drops real heterozygous carriers in lookup_by_genotype. See issues #538 / #491.
_provenance.coverage_note: Seeded with the loci verified for #538; rs2858884 added (#608) once re-keyed to its real A/C frame; rs144012689 added (#709) to guard the HLA-B*15:02 T/A frame. Bi-allelic-in-Ensembl loci additionally get a real-het-resolves check. Extend this map (one Ensembl GRCh37 lookup per rsid) to cover every categorical-panel locus (128 unique loci); the follow-up to #538 (#608) tracks full coverage.
_provenance.blind_spot_note: The set-based strand check is UNDECIDABLE for some multi-allelic loci: when every curated allele's Watson-Crick complement is also a real allele, a mixed-strand pair false-passes. rs9273363 (HLA-DQB1, C/A/G) is the case — panel {C, T} complements to {G, A}, both real, so it reads 'consistent' even though C is plus-strand and T is the complement of A; and being tri-allelic it gets no bi-allelic het-resolves check either. Such loci need an observed-genotype-resolves check, not the allele-set check; rs9273363 is therefore NOT listed here and is tracked separately (its T1D risk direction is also contested). rs4341 (4-allelic) has the same undecidability.
